author: diagrammo
description: 40+ diagram types in your notes from one plain-text language. Process, architecture, data,   project, hierarchy — diffable, searchable, mobile-friendly, with live preview and themed  palettes. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Diagrammo
manifest.description: Render diagrams inline from plain-text dgmo code blocks. 40+ chart types — flowcharts, sequence, gantt, charts, mind maps, and more — with live preview, themed palettes, and mobile support.
manifest.name: Diagrammo Diagrams

name: Diagrammo Diagrams

releases.author: demian0311
releases.description:
**In-block CodeMirror 6 editor with Vim support.** Open a diagram's source panel to edit it in a real CodeMirror 6 editor with dgmo language token highlighting and — when Obsidian's Vim mode is on — full Vim keybindings. Transparent overlay, silent autosave, no commit ceremony.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/diagrammo/obsidian-dgmo/compare/1.15.0...1.16.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/diagrammo/obsidian-dgmo/compare/1.14.0...1.15.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/diagrammo/obsidian-dgmo/compare/1.13.3...1.14.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 🐛 Fix: caret misaligned in the live editor

Obsidian's built-in code-block padding shifted the highlighted text a few pixels off the caret position. Both editor layers now share pinned, identical text metrics — the caret sits exactly on the glyphs.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/diagrammo/obsidian-dgmo/compare/1.13.2...1.13.3

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 🐛 Fix: source vanished on hover in the live editor

Obsidian's built-in `textarea:hover` styling painted an opaque background over the highlight layer, hiding the code whenever the mouse was over the editor. Editor states are now pinned transparent (theme-proof).

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/diagrammo/obsidian-dgmo/compare/1.13.1...1.13.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 🎨 Syntax highlighting while you edit

The live source editor (new in 1.13.0) now keeps DGMO syntax highlighting as you type — same colors as the read-only source view, with the diagram still re-rendering live above.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/diagrammo/obsidian-dgmo/compare/1.13.0...1.13.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## ✏️ Live source editing

Open a diagram's source panel (the `</>` icon under any diagram) and just start typing — **the diagram re-renders live as you edit**, right there in your note. No modes, no buttons: when you click away, your changes are saved back into the note's markdown automatically.

- Live preview while typing, with inline error feedback that recovers as you fix the source
- Autosaves on blur, panel close, or scrolling away — Esc reverts your draft
- Tab indents (DGMO is indentation-aware)
- Safe write-back: if the note changed underneath the editor, your draft is preserved instead of clobbering it

`![[*.dgmo]]` file embeds remain read-only.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/diagrammo/obsidian-dgmo/compare/1.12.2...1.13.0

releases.author: demian0311
releases.description:
## Fixed

- **Pyramid diagrams no longer clip their right-column descriptions.** Bumps `@diagrammo/dgmo` to 0.25.3, whose embed bounding-box estimator now honors `text-anchor` — previously start-anchored description text was under-measured and clipped when the diagram scaled to fit the note width.
- Edit-link icon repositioned to the bottom-right so it no longer overlaps Obsidian's edit-block button.

## Changed

- Tightened the README and plugin description; removed the awkward inline `` ```dgmo `` code-pill from the intro.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/diagrammo/obsidian-dgmo/compare/1.5.21...1.5.22

releases.author: demian0311
releases.description:
### Fixed

- **Word-cloud diagrams no longer collapse to a single giant clipped title** in the reading view. Bumped `@diagrammo/dgmo` to 0.25.2: the embed normalizer no longer trusts a measured content box that covers less than half the canvas (word-cloud words are transform-positioned and invisible to the string bbox estimator, so only the title was measured).


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/diagrammo/obsidian-dgmo/compare/1.5.20...1.5.21

releases.author: demian0311
releases.description:
### Fixed

- **Embedded diagrams no longer clip in the reading view.** Wide/tall diagrams (ER tables, mindmap nodes, flowchart elements) were being cut off at the right/bottom edge because the shared embed normalizer could override a diagram's correct viewBox with an out-of-bounds estimate. Bumped `@diagrammo/dgmo` to 0.25.1, which only tightens the viewBox when it's a genuine sub-rectangle of the diagram's canvas.
- **Map diagrams now render mountain relief.** The `mountain-ranges` geo asset is now bundled into the plugin; relief is on by default for maps in dgmo 0.25.x, but the asset wasn't shipped, so Obsidian maps rendered flat.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/diagrammo/obsidian-dgmo/compare/1.5.19...1.5.20

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Bundles `@diagrammo/dgmo@^0.17.0` — [sequence participant types trimmed from 9 → 4 + default](https://github.com/diagrammo/dgmo/releases/tag/v0.17.0).

### Breaking surface for users

Notes containing DGMO sequence diagrams with `is a service` / `is a frontend` / `is a networking` / `is a gateway` / `is a external` will surface `E_PARTICIPANT_TYPE_REMOVED` after the plugin updates. Drop the override — the participant renders as the default rectangle.

### Internal sweep

- Bundled example note's checkout flow no longer carries removed-type overrides on `App` or `Stripe`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

Picks up **[@diagrammo/dgmo 0.16.0](https://github.com/diagrammo/dgmo/releases/tag/v0.16.0)** with the new universal trailing-token color syntax:

```
Sales red
Done green
```

Color a label by appending a lowercase color name (`red`, `orange`, `yellow`, `green`, `blue`, `purple`, `teal`, `cyan`, `gray`, `black`, `white`) as the trailing token. Capitalize (`Red`) to use a color word as a literal label. Parentheses in labels are now literal text, not color notation.

The legacy `Label (color)` form has been removed — diagrams using it will need to be updated. See the [dgmo 0.16.0 release notes](https://github.com/diagrammo/dgmo/releases/tag/v0.16.0) for full migration details.

## Install

The Obsidian community plugin store will pick this up automatically within a few hours. For BRAT users: the version is `1.5.14`.

## What's Changed

- Bump @diagrammo/dgmo to ^0.16.0
- Rebuild main.js
